description: Real incident validation dataset for CyberScale pipeline. Each incident is extracted from RETEX analysis in the Obsidian vault with documented actual outcomes.

schema.name: Incident name
schema.country: ISO 2-letter country code (LU, NL, PL, EU)
schema.incident_data: CyberScale input fields extracted from RETEX
schema.actual_outcomes: What actually happened — ground truth for validation
schema.expected_cyberscale: Expected CyberScale outputs at each pipeline layer
schema.notes: Analyst notes on mapping decisions or ambiguities

incidents.name: POST Luxembourg Cyberattack

incident_data.description: Cyberattack exploiting Huawei router vulnerability causes 3.5-hour nationwide telecom outage at POST Luxembourg. Emergency services 112/113 access via POST compromised. Airport, banking, and government services disrupted.

actual_outcomes.crisis_mechanism: Prime Minister convened governmental crisis cell at CNC Senningen
actual_outcomes.notable: Single-operator emergency service dependency. Led to PGGCCN framework update.

entity_significance.comment: POST is a public electronic communications provider — may route to IR thresholds or LU national depending on entity type mapping

incidents.notes: Emergency services 112/113 accessibility was compromised but no deaths reported. Government crisis cell was activated in reality. POST entity type may map to public_electronic_communications_provider (IR Art. 14 would not apply — that's trust services). safety_impact=none because no actual health outcomes, but the emergency service disruption was the primary concern. No fast-track: data_impact=none (service disruption, not data breach) so fast-track unauthorized access condition not met. C2 met via service interruption sub-criterion instead.

incidents.name: LuxTrust Outage December 2025

incident_data.description: Network card failure triggers latent software bug under high transaction volume at LuxTrust. 29-hour total disruption of trust services. Digital banking, government portals, municipal services, education paralyzed across Luxembourg.

actual_outcomes.notable: 12 financial entities filed DORA notifications. Hardware failure, not malicious. Trust service provider has strictest thresholds (20min).

entity_significance.comment: Trust service provider — IR Art. 14 thresholds. 29h >> 20min threshold. Clearly significant.

incidents.notes: No crisis activation in reality despite 29h national disruption. HCPN crisis qualification depends on coordination_required and urgent_decisions_required — these are analyst judgment inputs. Setting to null (uncertain) matches reality: it was borderline. The module should recommend consultation. Not malicious → no fast-track.

incidents.name: Proximus Luxembourg (Tango) Network Outage

incident_data.description: Ethernet switch hardware failure in Proximus Luxembourg core network. 25-50% of mobile customers affected for 4 hours. Emergency services accessible via other operators.

actual_outcomes.crisis_mechanism: CERC convened for risk assessment only
actual_outcomes.notable: Occurred 13 days after POST attack. Heightened sensitivity. CERC consulted but no crisis activation.

entity_significance.comment: Degraded service (not unavailable), non-malicious. Likely below NIS2 significant threshold.

incidents.notes: Clear non-qualifying case. Hardware failure, partial impact, no malicious intent, no coordination needed. CERC consultation was precautionary (13 days post-POST).

incidents.name: CTIE DDoS Attack

incident_data.description: DDoS attack on public.lu domain. Guichet.lu, CNS portal, and state websites offline for 41 minutes.

actual_outcomes.notable: 41-minute DDoS. Third critical incident in 6 months (POST, LuxTrust, CTIE). Suspected pro-Russian hacktivist (NoName057(16)).

entity_significance.comment: Public administration entity — NIS2 ML qualitative assessment. Short duration DDoS.

incidents.notes: Essential service affected (public_administration) and malicious, but no data access (DDoS only) so no fast-track. No coordination or urgency needed for 41-minute DDoS. C3 fails.

incidents.name: CTIE Malware on MDM System

incident_data.description: Malware detected on CTIE Mobile Device Management platform. Suspected CVE-2026-1281 (Ivanti EPMM). Mobile access to state services disabled. Desktop access unaffected. Investigation ongoing.

actual_outcomes.notable: Investigation ongoing. Parallel to NL Ivanti exploitation. If CVE-2026-1281 confirmed, fast-track provision would apply.

entity_significance.comment: Public administration, malicious compromise, degraded service. Likely significant.

incidents.notes: Borderline case. Essential service (public_admin) affected. C2: national security (public_admin sector) + sensitive data (government_data) are deterministically met. But service_impact=degraded (not unavailable) so no fast-track. C3 coordination/urgency unknown → undetermined → recommend consultation. In reality no crisis was activated but investigation was ongoing.

incidents.name: NL Government Ivanti EPMM Exploitation
incidents.source: 03_Analysis/06_Recover/Incident-Analysis/NL/2026/2026-02-12 - NL Government Ivanti EPMM Exploitation - RETEX.md

incident_data.description: CVE-2026-1281 zero-day exploitation affecting 7 Dutch government organizations. 5 months undetected access at DJI (prison service). Personal data of government employees exfiltrated.

actual_outcomes.crisis_mechanism: Standard incident response, no interministerial coordination reported
actual_outcomes.notable: 5 months dwell time. 86 global instances. Parallel exploitation in LU (CTIE) and EU Commission. Zero-day → mass exploitation within hours of PoC.

entity_significance.comment: NL entity — no national module for NL. NIS2 ML qualitative assessment. Clearly significant (malicious, data exfiltrated, government).

hcpn_crisis.comment: NL incident — HCPN does not apply (Luxembourg framework only). Included for cross-reference with CTIE-MALWARE and to test the pipeline handles non-LU incidents correctly.

incidents.notes: HCPN crisis qualification does not apply to NL incidents. Included in dataset for: (1) entity significance validation via NIS2 ML, (2) cross-reference with LU CTIE-MALWARE (same CVE), (3) demonstrating that the HCPN tool is scoped to LU impact.

incidents.name: Poland Energy Sector Cyberattack

incident_data.description: Coordinated destructive cyberattack by Russian state actors on Polish energy infrastructure. 30+ wind/solar farms and major CHP plant targeted. Custom wiper malware (DynoWiper, LazyWiper) deployed against IT and OT systems. RTUs and IEDs permanently damaged.

actual_outcomes.notable: First major attack on distributed energy resources. 9 months dwell time. OT-capable state adversary. Destructive intent (wiper, no ransom). NATO member targeting.

entity_significance.comment: PL entity — no national module for PL. NIS2 ML qualitative assessment. Energy sector, destructive attack, clearly significant.

hcpn_crisis.comment: PL incident — HCPN does not apply. But if analogous attack hit LU energy grid, it would clearly qualify.

incidents.notes: HCPN crisis qualification does not apply to PL incidents. Included for: (1) entity significance validation, (2) demonstrating what an energy sector attack looks like with real parameters, (3) as a template for hypothetical LU energy attack scenarios.

incidents.name: European Commission AWS Breach

incident_data.description: Supply-chain compromise of Trivy CI/CD tool leads to AWS API key theft. 5-day undetected dwell time. 91.7 GB data exfiltrated from Europa hosting platform. 71+ affected clients (42 Commission + 29 other EU entities).

actual_outcomes.notable: EU institution — formally under Regulation 2023/2841, not NIS2. Supply-chain compromise vector. ShinyHunters published leaked data.

entity_significance.comment: EU institution entity — NIS2 ML qualitative assessment. Data exfiltrated, government data, supply-chain attack. Significant.

hcpn_crisis.comment: EU-level incident — HCPN does not apply directly. But if exfiltrated data included Luxembourg government data, HCPN could be triggered by LU authority for the LU impact portion.

incidents.notes: HCPN does not apply to EU institution incidents directly. Included for: (1) entity significance validation, (2) supply-chain attack pattern, (3) cross-border impact demonstration, (4) potential LU angle if Luxembourg government data was among the 91.7 GB exfiltrated.

incidents.name: Stryker Wiper Attack

incident_data.description: Iran-linked hacktivist group Handala (MOIS-affiliated Void Manticore) executed destructive wiper attack on Stryker Corporation via weaponised Microsoft Intune remote wipe. ~80,000 devices wiped across 79 countries between 05:00-08:00 UTC using compromised domain admin credentials from infostealer logs. No traditional malware — living-off-the-land via management plane. Global operations shut down for 22 days. 5,500+ workers sent home in Ireland alone.

actual_outcomes.crisis_mechanism: CISA and FBI engaged with Stryker executives; FBI seized two Handala websites
actual_outcomes.notable: First major cyber retaliation against US corporation during US-Israel-Iran conflict. Living-off-the-land via Intune — no malware deployed. Infostealer-harvested credentials available on dark web for months/years. SEC Form 8-K filed same day. Medical devices confirmed safe. Full restoration 22 days (2026-04-02). Geopolitical motivation: 2019 acquisition of Israeli OrthoSpace + US DoD contracts.

entity_significance.comment: US entity with EU operations (Ireland hub). Manufacturing sector (medical devices) — NIS2 Annex II. NIS2 ML qualitative assessment for EU entities. Financial loss clearly exceeds EUR 500K (3 weeks disruption at $22.6B company). Malicious access causing severe disruption. Significant.

hcpn_crisis.comment: US incident with EU/Ireland impact — HCPN does not apply (Luxembourg framework only). Ireland operations affected but no Luxembourg-specific impact documented. If Stryker had Luxembourg operations or supplied Luxembourg hospitals, HCPN could be triggered for the LU healthcare supply chain impact.

incidents.notes: US-headquartered, HCPN not applicable. Included for: (1) entity significance validation — manufacturing/health cross-sector, (2) living-off-the-land attack pattern via management plane (no malware), (3) state-actor attribution (Iran/MOIS via Handala/Void Manticore), (4) supply chain impact on healthcare facilities, (5) geopolitical context for threat modelling, (6) 22-day recovery timeline reference for wiper scenarios. medical_device_manufacturer may not be in nis2_entity_types.json — map to closest Annex II manufacturing entity type.

incidents.name: Odido Data Breach

incident_data.description: Cybercriminals breached Odido (largest NL mobile telecom, ~7M customers) customer contact system. 6.2 million customer records exfiltrated (89% of customer base) including names, addresses, IBANs, dates of birth, and identity document numbers. Operational services not affected. Attackers contacted Odido to claim theft but no group publicly claimed responsibility.

actual_outcomes.notable: 89% of customer base compromised. IBAN + identity document numbers create persistent identity theft risk. Free SAS precedent: EUR 42M fine for similar breach (24M subscribers). No service disruption — pure data exfiltration. Reported to Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens. Customer notification within 48h.

entity_significance.comment: NL telecom provider — NIS2 Annex I digital infrastructure. Malicious unauthorized access + 6.2M persons affected + data exfiltrated. Clearly significant under NIS2 Art. 23 horizontal criteria (malicious access causing severe harm).

hcpn_crisis.comment: NL incident — HCPN does not apply (Luxembourg framework only).

incidents.notes: NL incident, HCPN not applicable. Included for: (1) entity significance validation — pure data breach with no service disruption, (2) large-scale personal data exfiltration (6.2M records including IBAN + ID documents), (3) telecom sector breach pattern (cf. Free SAS EUR 42M fine, SK Telecom 27M), (4) NIS2 horizontal criteria test: significant via malicious access + damage to other persons, not via service disruption, (5) GDPR notification compliance reference (AP notified, customers within 48h). sensitive_data_type=null because exposed data is personal/financial, not government_data/industrial_secrets/critical_strategic_data per HCPN taxonomy.
